keeper has combat training. they're not very good at it so dont count on it
arc2 wise, keeper uses stands
Keeper has enough combat training to lose to Ramona and that’s about all they ever established

Ramona was training to join the royal knights in case shit went sideways, but they don’t really establish what that means. I guess Lotan was satisfied with her performance because she doesn’t negatively comment on it much.
not very good but seems usable enough tho <:Brown_think:1459080117308620996>
it might help to use william as the measuring stick in his case
lotan teaches swordplay, so it can be assumed all investigators know basics in that
and williams and ramonas styles are similar in the sense that they serve 2 primary functions: self defense and breaching enemy lines
it can be inferred that mythag style combat is less about winning, and more about just making openings to survive
this can be compared to MTC style combat which is more offensive and can switch between crowd-control and single target restraining

theres also the point that investigators are usually paired with an awakener during fieldwork (william is just weird) so they're also more dependant on commanding the awakener in combat scenarios anyway instead of doing most of the rough work themselves

given keepers talents, it can be argued that they were given a bit of slack in combat class in exchange for more tactics and strategy lessons, since commanding multiple people requires a different type of skillset
the Special Training wheel does provide quite a bit of insight into keepers physical abilities
also i just realized this is Sorel casually chatting ||with N, nonchalantly||

Do we know how one (Mythag or MTC) to spot a person be an Awakener or human?
Because if they do (and if we do), William may have a legit excuse to not have an awakener in field work

Another possible reason
As awakener just 'awaken' may go mad, if he have an awakener with him, containing said berserked awakener may or may not feasible, so having himself always available for linking is the safer option <:Nautila_think:1459077762684948500>
based on salv, its quite easy for some awakeners to get away with being human unless they use their abilities, hence the entire registration system
and by normal procedures, awakeners should only be awakened within campus grounds, so this is a very unlikely scenario
any stray awakeners outside mythag would either already be linked, or would have drawn enough attention for MTC to take action first
in either scenario MTC would override mythag so an unlinked investigator would not be required

That's right, let the nice lady with a sword go into the orphanage. I'm sure she's allowed to have that
ah icic

*used to be in law school* depend on the code (of law), (registered) specialists of some professions are allowed to carry blade objects-that-is-used-as-tool-of-trade-purpose, like a kebab chef still can carry knives even if it can be used for other intentions, tho you need a permit for that
*french law btw but the logic is similar to other laws*

also there are some weapons that are similar to sword but counted as knife <:Brown_think:1459080117308620996>

I have finished Ch6 few days ago, and just now finished reading Miryam's Dreamscape.

Still has few questions left unanswered and would like to know whether another side story or following chapters cover them, I missed the answer entirely or it's left vague.

1.|| What's the nature of the "Divine Festus" in the end? What she picked up in ruins clearly wasn't a silver core. I remember Tulu mentioned it's of the same nature as the Dream he used to look into, but that doesn't explain much.||
2. Is there an explanation why she, and by extension Goliath, became playable? Or is it on "Just because it's cool to add villains" basis?
3. ||How could Miryam survive for so long without a linker, and even be able to link awakeners to her?||

To answer 2. 
||We got her silver and brought it back. After we reawakened her she came to terms with reality and believes tulu is indeed the real tulu, so she’s calmer now, albeit obsessed with him. But yes she’s likely under strict watch from mythag to not do any ritual sacrifice and such||

To answer 3?
We have no idea lmaooo she’s just like that
Where is it stated? In main story chapters after 6th or another side story?

I don’t remember if it’s stated in her stories or just implied through her voicelines, but she very clearly believes tulu is indeed the real one now, and she stays at mythag, so the rest can kind of be implied regardless
Either way, it doesn’t happen in any story or cutscene no

answering 1
|| the "divine fetus" was believed by miryam to have been a way to bring tulu/her god back, as his reborn self after (as she believed) everyone died after Lemuria's sinking.||
idk how much else you know (have you read coronation day/tulu side story?) so i won't expand on more

||I know what Miryam *thought * it was, but that's not what it actually is.||

Admittedly she isn’t the first awakener we’ve seen to be able to do whatever they want without a linker, but those are very very rare. And I don’t think we know of any awakeners who can link other awakeners like she can
Perhaps she is so strong mentally (as absurd as it sound to say about a madwoman) that she the insanity possessing awakeners without a linker can't take hold of her
She’s definitely resolute and unshakable in her beliefs at least, that’s true
But that's just me speculating. Either way, that's a plothole left unclosed...
But yeah, no clue
Now, I just gotta pray to get her from standard banner
what if she's already insane though?
She’s too cold and calculating, the insanity that claims awakeners is more akin to a frenzy from what we’ve seen
true, but i mean it's still a possibility
She’s a crazy woman, sure. But she’s certainly still in her stable state of mind at most times. Even when linked at mythag her attitude doesn’t really change at all

Closest example I can think of for weird awakener shenanigans is || Xu|| who doesn’t even appear in the main story, but everything we see in her deep dive, story and conversation dialogue just shows she’s a weird case. Yet even *then* you can explain it in a sense due to her backstory. Miryam is kinda just unexplainable in comparison
